ORDER DENYING DEFENDANTS AND CROSS-COMPLAINANTS CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF GENERAL SERVICES AND FRED KLASS' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND ANSWER TO CALIFORNIA FIRST, LP'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF GENERAL SERVICES AND FRED KLASS' MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND ANSWER TO CALIFORNIA FIRST LP'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. DENIED. "[C]OURTS ARE MUCH MORE CRITICAL OF PROPOSED AMENDMENTS TO ANSWERS WHEN OFFERED'ON THE EVE OF TRIAL." (HULSEY V. KOEHLER (1990) 218 CAL.APP.3D 1150, 1159.) "WHERE INEXCUSABLE DELAY AND PROBABLE PREJUDICE TO THE OPPOSING PARTY IS SHOWN," DENIAL OF LEAVE TO AMEND IS APPROPRIATE. (MAGPALI V. FARMERS GROUP, INC. (1996) 48 CAL.APP.4TH 471, 487.) EVEN IF IT IS ASSUMED THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT DISCOVER THE EMAIL UNTIL APRIL 2014, THE EVIDENCE ESTABLISHES THAT DEFENDANT HAS INEXCUSABLY DELAYED IN BRINGING THIS MOTION. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL ADMITS THAT HE BECAME AWARE OF THE EMAILS CONTAINING PURPORTED MISREPRESENTATIONS IN APRIL 2014. (DECLARATION OF LANDON D. BAILEY, PARS. 3-5.) "IN ORDER TO TEST THE VERACITY OF THESE WRITTEN REPRESENTATIONS" DEFENDANT NOTICED THE DEPOSITIONS OF PERSONS MOST KNOWLEDGEABLE AT THE INVESTMENT FIRMS. (ID., PARS. 5-8.) THESE DEPOSITIONS CONCLUDED IN MID-AUGUST. (ID., PARS. 6-8.) DEFENDANT OFFERS NO EXPLANATION OF WHY IT WAITED UNTIL SEPTEMBER 30, 2014, OVER A MONTH AFTER THE DEPOSITIONS CONCLUDED AND JUST TWO WEEKS BEFORE TRIAL, TO SEEK LEAVE TO AMEND ITS ANSWER. FURTHER, PLAINTIFF HAS SHOWN THAT IT WILL BE UNDULY PREJUDICED IF LEAVE TO AMEND IS GRANTED. (SEE DECLARATION OF EDWARD KLEIN, PARS. 13-14.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR# 5976.(302/EHG)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Leave To Amend Answer To California First Lp'S Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-09-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Andrew Stroud, Esq. of Hanson Bridgett, T:916.491.3052, for defendants and moving party;
Edward A. Klein, Esq. of Liner LLP, T:310.500.3569, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. After argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS CALIFORNIA DEPARTMENT OF GENERAL SERVICES and FRED KLASS' Motion For Leave To Amend Answer To California First LP'S Second Amended Complaint. Denied. "[C]ourts are much more critical of proposed amendments to answers when offered'on the eve of trial." (Hulsey v. Koehler (1990) 218 Cal.App.3d 1150, 1159.) "Where inexcusable delay and probable prejudice to the opposing party is shown," denial of leave to amend is appropriate. (Magpali v. Farmers Group, Inc. (1996) 48 Cal.App.4th 471, 487.) Even if it is assumed that Defendant did not discover the email until April 2014, the evidence establishes that Defendant has inexcusably delayed in bringing this motion. Defendant's counsel admits that he became aware of the emails containing purported misrepresentations in April 2014. (Declaration of Landon D. Bailey, pars. 3-5.) "In order to test the veracity of these written representations" Defendant noticed the depositions of Persons Most Knowledgeable at the investment firms. (Id., pars. 5-8.) These depositions concluded in mid-August. (Id., pars. 6-8.) Defendant offers no explanation of why it waited until September 30, 2014, over a month after the depositions concluded and just two weeks before trial, to seek leave to amend its answer. Further, Plaintiff has shown that it will be unduly prejudiced if leave to amend is granted. (See Declaration of Edward Klein, pars. 13-14.) ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Denied. Plaintiff is unlikely to prevail on the merits for the reasons set forth in the above tentative ruling. Additionally, there has been no illegal delegation of authority. The legislature was specific in its delegation, identified specific buildings, and stated the price of fair market value or greater. The legislature required defendant to report back before the deal was consummated. See Salmon Trollers Marketing Association, Inc. v. Fullerton (1981) 124 Cal App 3d 291. The "Best interests of the state" direction is permissible. Insufficient showing that the balancing of hardships favors plaintiffs. Letter, titled Golden State Asset Acquisition Group, submitted by Intervenor to the Court as exhibit 1 to Mr. Casper's presentation (letter attached as exhibit to declaration of Louise H. Renne filed 12/10/10, after hearing). Order, approved as to form, signed after hearing. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
